The alleged total, $1,024,298.09. earned $511,318.90 … $27, ...
Just days after turning 18, internet star Lil Tay says she earned over $1 million within her first three hours on OnlyFans — a claim that, if true, would mark a record-breaking debut.
On Sunday, August 3, the viral teen influencer, real name Claire Hope, shared a screenshot of what she says is her earnings from the adult content platform.
If confirmed by the platform, Lil Tay would be dethroning a previous record held by rapper Bhad Bhabie, who claimed she made $1 million in six hours after launching her own OnlyFans in 2021.
In her post, Lil Tay included a detailed screenshot breaking down the alleged revenue from her OnlyFans debut, in which she earned $511,318.90 from subscriptions, $486,381.19 from private messages and $27,405 in tips.
Initial reactions on social media have been mixed, with some supporting the move and others questioning the ethics of the launch.
This story was originally reported by Parade on Aug 5, 2025, where it first appeared."},{"url":"https://www.complex.com/pop-culture/a/kris-seavers/sophie-rain-doesnt-believe-lil-tay-made-15-million-two-weeks-onlyfans","title":"Sophie Rain Doesn't Believe Lil Tay Made $15 Million in Two Weeks on OnlyFans","content":"OnlyFans star Sophie Rain thinks Lil Tay is exaggerating about making $15 million in two weeks on thr platform.
Tay, legal name is Claire Eileen Qi Hope, turned 18 over the summer and immediately launched her OnlyFans account, claiming that she made $1 million in the first three hours.
Last week, Tay posted on Instagram that her recent earnings totaled $15 million and claimed that she had raked in the sum in only two weeks, making her the “No.
Rain, 20, immediately doubted the $15 million claim, telling TMZ she found it hard to believe the former rapper could have made that much in two weeks.
3), Page Six published an interview with Tay, the former “youngest flexer of the century,” who doubled down on the claim that she had pulled the $15 million in the span of two weeks.
